evidence_quotes.quote: Developers in today’s industry have the ability to withdraw from games that you have already bought.
Thanks to the fact that the games you buy today are fully digital and many others require a stable internet connection, games have been taken offline.
I would like to see the Digital Fairness Act counter this trend and ask developers to have a plan for what happens after a game is taken offline.

paragraphs.text: Dear, I support what the Digital Fairness Act is trying to do. But I wonder whether it is possible to expand its range slightly to deal with a trend in the computer spel industry. Developers in today’s industry have the ability to withdraw from games that you have already bought. Thanks to the fact that the games you buy today are fully digital and many others require a stable internet connection, games have been taken offline. Customers in these scenarios simply lose access to a game they paid for. I would like to see the Digital Fairness Act counter this trend and ask developers to have a plan for what happens after a game is taken offline. Thank you!
